seoTitle: How to Upload a PHP Project to Hostinger Hosting 2026
metaDescription: Ready to deploy your PHP project on Hostinger? Here's how to upload it via File Manager or FTP, set up a database, and avoid common launch issues.
intro: Getting a PHP project live on Hostinger comes down to placing your files in the right directory and connecting a database if your project needs one. This guide covers uploading through both Hostinger's File Manager and an FTP client like FileZilla, plus the permissions and setup details beginners tend to trip over. The goal is a working deployment without technical guesswork.

steps.heading: Choose your upload method
steps.body: Decide between Hostinger's built-in File Manager for a browser-based upload or an FTP client like FileZilla for transferring larger projects.

steps.heading: Upload your PHP files to the correct directory
steps.body: Place your project files inside the public-facing directory Hostinger uses for your domain, so the site is reachable once uploaded.

steps.heading: Set up a database if your project needs one
steps.body: If your PHP project relies on a database, create it through Hostinger's hosting panel and update your project's connection settings to match.

steps.heading: Check file permissions and common issues
steps.body: Review file and folder permissions after uploading, since incorrect permissions are a frequent cause of PHP projects failing to load properly.

steps.heading: Verify the site is live
steps.body: Visit your domain to confirm the PHP project loads correctly and that any database-driven pages are pulling data as expected.

faq.question: Should I use File Manager or FTP to upload a PHP project?
faq.answer: Either works — File Manager is quicker for smaller projects directly in the browser, while an FTP client like FileZilla is often better suited for larger projects with many files.

faq.question: Do I need a database for every PHP project on Hostinger?
faq.answer: No, only projects that rely on stored data need a database set up — simpler PHP scripts can run without one.

faq.question: Why isn't my PHP site loading after upload?
faq.answer: This is often a permissions issue or a file placed in the wrong directory, both of which are covered as common problems beginners run into during deployment.

faq.question: How long does it take to get a PHP project live on Hostinger?
faq.answer: The video frames this as a quick deployment process that can be completed in just a few minutes once your files and any needed database are ready.
